question: how do I know if my baby has a good latch?
notes: Verbatim from LAT-001 variants. Sanity check — should always pass; regression here means embedding pipeline broke.

question: is my latch deep enough or is baby just on the nipple?
notes: Paraphrase not in variants — tests whether embeddings generalize beyond the seed phrasings.

question: my nipple looks all squished and weird after she eats, is something wrong?
notes: Colloquial phrasing of 'lipstick nipple'. New moms rarely use clinical terms — this slice catches over-tuning to FAQ wording.

question: my breast is red hot and I feel like I have the flu, do I have a breast infection?
notes: Mastitis symptom description. Should trigger escalation_text since fever + breast redness is a 'call IBCLC/PCP' case. Update expect_escalation once the CSV row is confirmed.

question: how do I tell if it's just a plugged duct or actual mastitis?
notes: Confusable pair test. BRS-002 (mastitis) and BRS-003 (mastitis vs. duct) both score high — BRS-003 must rank first because the user is explicitly asking for the distinction.

question: my nipples are cracked and bleeding and the baby keeps unlatching, what do I do?
expected_sub_questions: my nipples are cracked and bleeding
notes: Classic compound: pain + behavior. Decomposer must split into 2 atomic Qs; both should match HIGH; no contradiction because they address different aspects.

question: how many ounces should I pump and why did my output suddenly drop this week?
expected_sub_questions: how many ounces should I pump per session
why did my pump output drop this week
notes: Two questions in the same category — tests that dedup by faq_id doesn't collapse them.

question: what type of bra is best for breastfeeding?
notes: Bras at HIGH should still return. The downgrade rule only applies at MEDIUM range.

question: will my bra mess up my milk supply somehow
notes: Vague/borderline phrasing — cosine score likely lands in MEDIUM range against BRA-002. Bras category must downgrade MEDIUM→LOW and return matched:false. If this starts passing as HIGH, either the rule broke or the FAQ corpus grew enough that the score is legitimately HIGH (re-label).

question: what time is the next bus?
notes: Hard off-topic. Top cosine should be well under 0.65 → matched:false. Logged to unmatched_questions.

question: I think I might have postpartum depression, what should I do?
notes: Adjacent off-topic — emotionally loaded, semantically near 'emotional wellness' but not in scope of FAQ. Should return matched:false. Watch this one when adding Emotional Wellness FAQs — the right answer may flip from no-match to a real referral.

question: my wearable pump isn't getting much milk and I'm wondering if my flange size is wrong
expected_sub_questions: my wearable pump isn't emptying me fully
could the wrong flange size be the problem
notes: Cross-category compound (pump mechanics + flange fit). Tests that decomposer doesn't merge them into one query.

question: why am I getting way more in the morning than at night
notes: Diurnal supply variation — common real question, short, no jargon.

question: how do I know if baby is getting enough milk from just nursing?
notes: High-volume real-user question. Must rank above adjacent OUT-008 (diaper counts) and OUT-005 (newborn intake).

question: baby wants to nurse every 30 minutes all evening, is something wrong?
notes: Cluster feeding described without the term. If this matches a 'supply low' FAQ instead, that's a dangerous misroute — moms triple-feed unnecessarily.

question: my baby has flat nipples on the right side, will pumping help?
notes: Replace expected faq_id once the matching anatomy FAQ is identified. Test exists to verify the anatomy context injection in decomposeQuestion() actually shapes results — compare run-with vs run-without anatomy.

question: is this normal
notes: Pathologically vague. Top cosine will be incidental — must fall below 0.65 LOW threshold. Guards against the function returning *anything* for ambiguous input.

question: can a tight bra cause a clogged duct?
notes: Bras HIGH case — should pass through cleanly, no downgrade. Companion test to GS-009.

question: i have a painful white spot on the tip of my nipple
notes: Milk bleb. Must NOT match BRS-005 (thrush) or BRS-004 (cracked nipples) — three nearby FAQs in the same category, classic confusable-trio test.

question: baby's been nursing fine but I only have 4 wet diapers today, should I be worried?
notes: Borderline-concerning real scenario. Must match diaper-count FAQ rather than supply-anxiety FAQs. If escalation is wired to low-diaper-count, expect_escalation should be true once verified.
